title: nerve LSP server catalog
description: Committed catalog of language servers. Maps file extensions to servers nerve spawns over stdio (raw JSON-RPC). MULTIPLE servers may share an extension (e.g. Python uses pyrefly + ruff) — diagnostics aggregate from all, queries route to whichever advertises the capability. Servers start lazily on the first file of a matching type, are kept warm, and are killed on exit. nerve does NOT install servers; a missing one raises an actionable error (see `install`). Add a language = add an entry here.

$schema.description: Path to this schema file, enabling editor IntelliSense + validation.

servers.description: Language servers, matched to files by extension.

extensions.description: File extensions this server handles, each leading with a dot (e.g. ".ts", ".tsx").

command.description: Executable to spawn. Must be on PATH (nerve does not install servers).

install.description: Install command shown when the server isn't on PATH (e.g. "uv tool install pyrefly", "bun install -g @vtsls/language-server").

args.description: Arguments passed to the server. Most stdio servers need their stdio flag, e.g. ["--stdio"].

rootMarkers.description: Files that mark the project root for this server (e.g. ["tsconfig.json", "package.json"]). Used to compute the initialize rootUri. Defaults to the launch dir if omitted.

env.description: Extra environment variables for the server process.
